title: Kacey Musgraves and Kane Brown Lead New Australian Country Festival LONGHAUL
summary: The inaugural festival will stop in three Australian cities across one weekend.
body: Kane Brown and Kacey Musgraves are heading to Australia for the launch of LONGHAUL, a new country music festival arriving in January 2027. The inaugural festival will stop in three cities across one weekend, beginning Friday, Jan. 22, at Melbourne Showgrounds, before heading to Sydney Showground on Saturday, Jan. 23, and Brisbane RNA Showgrounds on Sunday, Jan. 24. Brown and Musgraves will headline the new event in what are billed as their only Australian shows. The lineup also features Charles Wesley Godwin, Margo Price, Maddox Batson, Ava Hall and James Barker Band, alongside Australian artists Beau Shearer, Denvah, Nathan Lamont, Taylor Moss and Tyla Rodrigues. Presented by LONGHAUL Touring and powered by TEG Live, the festival is entering the market at a time when country music’s Australian audience is becoming harder to ignore. The bill brings together arena-level U.S. names, Americana favorites, emerging international acts and local artists, pointing to a wider version of country than the genre’s traditional festival lane. “We have been working very hard on this festival concept for a number of years and so it is incredibly exciting to finally launch the inaugural LONGHAUL F…

title: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Is First TV Series Soundtrack in More Than a Decade to Achieve This Billboard 200 Feat
summary: The hit show's soundtrack skates onto multiple charts, including Soundtracks, Top Album Sales, Vinyl Albums, Indie Store Album Sales & the Billboard 200.
body: The soundtrack to the hit TV series Heated Rivalry debuts at No. 8 on the Billboard 200 albums chart (dated July 25) following the release of its CD and vinyl editions on July 10. It debuts with 41,000 equivalent album units (all from album sales) earned in the United States in the week ending July 17, according to Luminate. It also debuts atop the Vinyl Albums , Indie Store Album Sales and Soundtracks charts, and at No. 2 on Top Album Sales . All charts dated July 25 will post to Billboard’s website on July 21. Related Let’s Get Physical: ‘Heated Rivalry’ Announces Vinyl & CD Soundtrack Release for TV Show’s Viral Music ‘Heated Rivalry,’ ‘Jimmy Kimmel Live!’ & Programs on Sly Stone, Fela Kuti Nominated for 2026 Peabody Awards New Connor Storrie ‘Like a Prayer’ Lip-Sync Video Answers Fans’ Prayers for ‘Heated Rivalry’ Star’s Birthday Heated Rivalry marks the first soundtrack from a TV series to debut in the Billboard 200 top 10 in more than a decade. The last was the season 1 soundtrack to Fox’s Empire in 2015, which debuted at No. 1. Since Empire , the only other TV series s…

title: Official ‘KPop Demon Hunters’ Light Sticks Release Ahead of Global Tour — Here’s Where to Shop
summary: Great for concerts and sing-along viewings of the film alike, the light sticks feature five LED colors and more than eight lighting sequences.
body: All products and services featured are independently chosen by editors. However, Billboard may receive a commission on orders placed through its retail links, and the retailer may receive certain auditable data for accounting purposes. Light sticks have become one of the defining accessories at K-pop concerts, turning arenas into coordinated displays of color as fans wave synchronized lights throughout a show. With Netflix officially announcing the KPop Demon Hunters global concert tour, fans can start preparing by picking up the official HUNTR/X and Saja Boys light sticks, along with other themed accessories, ahead of the highly-anticipated shows. The fan tradition of waving light sticks during K-pop shows even made its way to this year’s Academy Awards during HUNTR/X’s performance of Golden . Audience members, including Emma Stone, Gwyneth Paltrow, Leonardo DiCaprio, Teyana Taylor and Chloé Zhao, were seen waving generic light sticks as the trio performed, bringing one of K-pop’s most recognizable concert traditions to Hollywood’s biggest stage. Now, you can get your hands on an official KPop Demon Hunters light stick online. OFFICIAL LIGHT STICK 'KP…

title: J. Cole Shocked After Bra Is Thrown Onstage During ‘Work Out’ Performance: ‘Gotta Cherish That Sh–‘
summary: The North Carolina rapper laughed and held the bra up to the crowd during a tour stop in Miami.
body: J. Cole ‘s  The Fall-Off World Tour has gotten off to an auspicious start. Last week during the tour’s second stop in Miami, a couple got engaged as he was performing “Life Sentence,” one of the latest cuts from his most recent album The Fall-Off , which is dedicated to love and marriage despite the song’s title. “What’s up, baby girl? What’s up, my boy? God bless y’all,” the rapper said once he caught wind of their engagement. “That’s f—ing beautiful. Y’all make some noise for them, man.” Related J. Cole Shouts Out Couple for Getting Engaged During ‘Life Sentence’ at His Show Darius Rucker Says He's Considering Retiring From Touring in Two Years Ariana Grande Sings Decade-Old Hit for the First Time in Years During Tech Difficulty at NYC Show There was also another couple who were getting married the day after the show at Miami’s Kaseya Center and had their seats upgraded by Cole’s team and invited him and his manager Ibrahim Hamad to the wedding. “Aye @KingOfQueenz @JColeNC we just wanna say thank you for tonight’s show in Miami. our original tickets had an ‘obstructed view’ and so y’all upgra…

title: PSY’s ‘Gangnam Style’ Becomes First K-Pop Video to Hit 6 Billion Views on YouTube
summary: The visual was uploaded 14 years ago.
body: Hey, sexy ladies! PSY ‘s smash hit “Gangnam Style” is now the first K-pop video to reach a major YouTube milestone. As of Monday (July 20) — almost exactly 14 years after it was posted on the platform — the humorous, dance-heavy visual has amassed more than 6 billion views. No other music video in the genre has reached those numbers to date, underscoring PSY’s legacy of setting the stage for modern K-pop to cross over into the global mainstream in recent years with acts such as BTS, BLACKPINK and more. Related Psy’s ‘Gangnam Style’ Turns 10: How Its Video Became the First Member of YouTube’s Billion Views Club Oppa! Psy’s ‘Gangnam Style’ Video Hits 5 Billion Views on YouTube Darius Rucker Says He's Considering Retiring From Touring in Two Years Directed by Cho Soo-Hyun, the video finds the South Korean superstar causing chaos wherever he goes with his signature “Gangnam Style” choreography. A number of now-iconic scenes emerged from the project, including PSY busting a move by himself in a horse stable or singing to the camera while lying between a dancing man’s legs. Rea…

title: Our First View of the Surface of Mars
summary: “Touchdown, we have touchdown!” At 5:12 a.m. PDT, July 20, 1976, mission controllers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory erupted in cheers as they learned that the Viking 1 lander had survived its descent through the thin Martian atmosphere. Forty minutes later, the lander’s first image began to ap…
body: On the morning of July 20, 1976, roughly 40 minutes after mission controllers received word that the Viking 1 lander had successfully touched down on the surface of Mars, this photo gave us our first view from the surface of another planet. NASA/JPL “Touchdown, we have touchdown!” At 5:12 a.m. PDT, July 20, 1976, mission controllers at NASA’s Jet Propulsion Laboratory erupted in cheers as they learned that the Viking 1 lander had survived its descent through the thin Martian atmosphere. Forty minutes later, the lander’s first image began to appear on their monitors, slowly forming line by line from left to right. For the first time, humans were able to see Mars’s rocky terrain from its surface. Dr. Thomas Mutch, leader of the Viking lander imaging team, described the moment: “I studied the black screen, waiting for that narrow strip that will signal the first few lines of the first picture. And it appeared. A sliver of electronic magic. Areas of brightness and darkness. The picture begins to fill the screen. Rocks and sand are visible and — finally at the far right — one of the spacecraft foot pads, a symbolic artifact that stamps our accomplishment with the sign of reality. Time …

title: Shaping the Emerald City
summary: The hills and basins of Seattle, Washington, have been sculpted by glaciers and modified by humans.
body: Earth Observatory Science Earth Observatory Shaping the Emerald City Earth Earth Observatory Image of the Day EO Explorer Topics All Topics Atmosphere Land Heat & Radiation Life on Earth Human Dimensions Natural Events Oceans Remote Sensing Technology Snow & Ice Water More Content Collections Global Maps World of Change Articles Earth Matters Blog Blue Marble: Next Generation EO Kids Mission: Biomes About About Us Subscribe 🛜 RSS Contact Us Search June 16, 2026 Seattle, Washington—sometimes known as the “Emerald City”—was glimmering in the morning sunlight when an astronaut aboard the International Space Station took this photo on June 16, 2026. The city’s parks and tree-filled neighborhoods lend a lush, green look to the metropolis, while tall buildings downtown cast long shadows and ships navigate surrounding waterways. The broad contours of the city’s landscape and the water around it owe their shape to the advance and retreat of glaciers during the last ice age. Between roughly 18,000 and 16,000 years ago, the Puget lobe of the Cordilleran ice sheet covered the area in a mass of ice up to 3,300 feet (1,000 meters) thick. The glacier scoured the basins now occupied b…
